intro: Cartrema matsumurana, also known as Japanese quinine or Cartrema, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cartrema matsumurana include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cartrema matsumurana is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to support overall health and well-being.
